filter_pushdown.note: Zone maps skip only ~2 tail blocks out of 600; main value is full columnar scan

group_by.rationale: 3 returnflag codes × 2 linestatus codes = 6 groups; direct flat array, no hashing needed

logical_plan.output_order: ORDER BY l_returnflag ASC, l_linestatus ASC — trivial sort on 6 rows at end

data_structures.aggregation: flat_array[6] of struct { double sum_qty, sum_base_price, sum_disc_price, sum_charge, sum_disc, sum_qty_for_avg, sum_price_for_avg; int64_t count; } — indexed by returnflag_code*2 + linestatus_code
data_structures.thread_local_accumulators: Each of 64 threads owns a private flat_array[6]; final merge is 64*6=384 double additions (sequential, negligible cost)
data_structures.zone_map: mmap indexes/lineitem_shipdate_zonemap.bin — uint32_t num_blocks + [int32_t min, int32_t max]*600

parallelism.synchronization: thread_local_merge — no shared state during scan; sequential merge after parallel phase
parallelism.note: Atomic adds unnecessary: each thread accumulates into private flat_array[6], merged serially at end

index_usage.purpose: skip_blocks_where_block_min_gt_10471 — eliminates last ~2 blocks (Sep–Dec 1998); minimal but zero-cost pruning

scan_detail.pass: One pass over lineitem blocks: for each row, check l_shipdate <= 10471, then read l_returnflag+l_linestatus codes, then fuse all 4 double columns (qty, price, disc, tax) into accumulator update. No intermediate materialization.
scan_detail.simd_opportunity: Batch filter on l_shipdate with AVX2 int32 compare; gather qualifying row indices for payload accumulation

physical_plan.memory_detail: All 7 columns read sequentially in block-aligned chunks; thread-local accumulators fit in L1 cache (6 slots × 64 bytes each = 384 bytes per thread)

io_strategy.rationale: HDD sequential bandwidth ~200 MB/s; 7 columns × ~480 MB each ≈ 3.3 GB total; parallel madvise MADV_WILLNEED issued concurrently for all columns to maximize disk throughput
io_strategy.prefetch_note: Issue madvise(MADV_WILLNEED) for all 7 data columns concurrently before scan begins; zone map is tiny and loaded first to enable selective block prefetch for future queries

post_processing.avg_computation: avg_qty = sum_qty / count; avg_price = sum_base_price / count; avg_disc = sum_disc / count — computed once per group after merge
post_processing.output_sort: std::sort on 6-element result array by (returnflag_code, linestatus_code); decode codes to strings via lookup vectors at output time

estimated_cost.dominant_phase: main_scan (memory-bandwidth-bound on warm cache; I/O-bound on cold HDD)
estimated_cost.bottleneck: Memory bandwidth: 3.3 GB / 64 threads; target ~84ms to match DuckDB/Umbra
